channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rising threats are driving local officials out of office, new California study shows
item.description: There has been a rise in threats made against local elected officials and candidates, prompting some to consider leaving their post or change their stance on a specific issue due to a 'climate of fear,' according to a recent study out of the University of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: US drops $10M terrorism bounty offered for capture of Syrian rebel leader who ousted Assad
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The Biden administration said Friday it has decided not to pursue a $10 million reward it had offered for the capture of a Syrian rebel leader whose forces led the ouster of President Bashar Assad earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada election leaders find nearly 200 cases of double votes from November
item.description: The Nevada Secretary of State’s Office is investigating 180 double votes from the 2024 general election, a report released Friday indicated.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden heading to Vatican City next month to meet with Pope Francis, Meloni in final overseas trip
item.description: The White House announced that President Joe Biden will head to Italy early next month for a public meeting with Pope Francis in Vatican City. During the trip he will also hold meetings with Italy's Prime Minister and President.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden canceling student loan debt for 55K public service workers
item.description: President Biden announced on Friday he would be canceling more student debt for tens of thousands of public service workers, possibly his last major move on the issue before leaving office in a month.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House starts scrapping pending regulations on transgender athletes and student debt
item.description: President Joe Biden’s administration has started to withdraw pending regulations that won’t be finalized by the time he leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Republicans scramble for funding plan C as shutdown deadline draws near
item.description: Congress is racing toward a shutdown at the end of the day Friday, and Republicans appear no closer to finding a path forward that will keep the lights on and appease President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Here's a list of the 38 Republicans who defied Trump by voting 'no' on the failed spending bill
item.description: A bill to avert a partial government shutdown that was backed by President-elect Trump failed to pass the House of Representatives on Thursday night, pushing Congress closer to a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A lynching in the family inspired Michigan's first Black woman elected justice to pursue the law
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — During Michigan Supreme Court Justice Kyra Harris Bolden's first campaign, a critic told her she wasn't Michelle Obama or Kamala Harris, “but you feel emboldened to run for this office.” She later named her first child Emerson, so it could be shortened to “Em Bolden.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In an early legislative test for Trump, plan B spending bill tanks in House
item.description: House Republicans failed to secure the votes needed Thursday on a spending bill amid a looming government shutdown, handing a loss to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Carter takes scalpel to St. Paul budget with line-item veto, approves smaller levy increase
item.description: St. Paul Mayor Melvin Carter used his line-item veto power to cut several items from the city’s 2025 budget, but the city council overrode all of the vetoes at a special meeting late Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems say 'hell no' to GOP plan B on government funding, debt ceiling
item.description: House Democrats are rallying in overwhelming opposition to the Republicans' second stab at a government funding bill, vowing to sink it on the House floor and force GOP leaders back to the table to negotiate a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutor Fani Willis is removed from the Georgia election case against Trump and others
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A state appeals court on Thursday removed Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others, the latest legal victory for the president-elect in criminal cases that once threatened his career and freedom.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: With a resounding no, the House rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s new plan Thursday to fund federal operations and suspend the debt ceiling a day before a government shutdown, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The history of White House Christmas trees, including Theodore Roosevelt's 'ban' of the holiday tradition
item.description: The reason for the absence of a White House Christmas tree during Theodore Roosevelt's presidency is unknown. Sources have various theories for why a Christmas tree was not displayed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: The Pentagon is warning that Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost more than 2 million troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday’s deadline to prevent a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Advocates seek to prepare for possible ‘mass deportations’ without fearmongering
item.description: Minnesota immigrant rights groups say they want to avoid evoking fear in their communities, but are finding ways to stay prepared ahead of the next Trump presidency.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans agree on Plan B government funding bill with Trump's debt limit increase
item.description: House Republicans have reached an agreement on funding the government before Friday's shutdown deadline. The deal would also suspend the debt limit for two years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans announce funding agreement, Thursday vote as shutdown looms
item.description: House Republicans have reached an agreement on a plan B to avert a government shutdown ahead of Friday’s deadline and plan to vote Thursday, lawmakers announced.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: ICE nabs several migrants convicted of child molestation, one convicted murderer, in blue state suburbs
item.description: Baltimore ICE Enforcement and Removal Operations officials arrested eight criminal illegal immigrants in suburban Maryland, including four convicted of child molestation and one murderer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shutdown odds rapidly rise as House seeks plan B
item.description: The odds of a shutdown were rapidly growing on Thursday after President-elect Trump upended the talks by urging GOP lawmakers to shutter the government unless Democrats agree to a clean stopgap spending bill that raises or abolishes the debt limit.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota hastens to regulate helium mining after deposit discovery near Babbitt
item.description: Minnesota is scrambling to figure out how to regulate a potential new industry — one discovered by accident — that is both extremely valuable and in demand.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: How much power would RFK Jr. have at HHS? A former health secretary weighs in
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is making the case with senators that he should lead Health and Human Services. Kathleen Sebelius, who had the job under Obama, explains the power and limits of the role.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gorenstein, MPR News

item.title: Party switch paying off? Valdes secures committee leadership after joining GOP
item.description: One state Representative in the Tampa Bay area made the controversial move to switch political parties, just last week. The news came was greeted with both cheers and criticisms.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pornhub to block access in Florida amid lawsuit over new age verification law
item.description: Pornhub said it will block access to its website from Florida as an adult entertainment advocacy group sues over the state's new law requiring age verification.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams expected in court on criminal charges: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota-backed bill to make bald eagles the national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: The bald eagle could soon become the national bird of the United States after a bill backed by Minnesota legislators passed the U.S. House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court for an emergency order to block a U.S. ban unless it’s sold
item.description: A similar plea was filed by content creators who rely on the platform for income and some of TikTok’s more than 170 million users in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former FBI informant will plead guilty to lying about phony bribery scheme involving the Bidens
item.description: A former FBI informant is set to plead guilty to lying about a phony bribery scheme involving President Joe Biden and his son, Hunter, that became central to a Republican impeachment inquiry in Congress.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Senator Schumer urges use of ROBIN to track mystery drones
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sen. Chuck Schumer is making a plea for federal officials to launch a declassified special drone detection system called the ROBIN – a 360-degree technology across New York, New Jersey and the metro area to track any unidentified object in the sky.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Mayor Adams seeks to deport migrants who commit crimes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams told President-elect Donald Trump’s border czar he would work with him to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City. Adams met with Tom Homan at Gracie Mansion to discuss Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
